page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this KVM-hypervised server features 64 dedicated physical cores with 1.0 thread per core running at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.g7a.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU tests. Cryptographic and extended instruction performance is highly efficient, as evidenced by strong PassMark encryption scores. Memory subsystem performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is in the top 25% at 4142.89 GB/sec, large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations perform exceptionally well, reaching 61.69 million operations per second.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated physical core allocation offers predictable compute performance without hyperthreading overhead, representing a solid value for CPU-bound workloads. However, the lack of bundled local storage requires users to provision external block storage, which introduces an architectural tradeoff. This server is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, database hosting, Redis caching layers, and moderate-scale LLM inference tasks using smaller models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.16xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at 2.55 GHz. It features 64 dedicated physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio and 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight strong multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, alongside high-performing database and Redis SET operations. Conversely, large-block uncached memory bandwidth and software compilation speeds remain in the average tier. Lacking local storage, this instance requires external block storage, making it a highly predictable and cost-efficient option for dedicated multi-threaded workloads, database hosting, and memory-cached applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core CPU and database benchmark performance.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.16xlarge features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 256 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core and database benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC server offering balanced memory-to-CPU allocation and strong multi-threaded performance for database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7T83 with 64 dedicated physical cores and 256 GB RAM.
Delivers strong top-tier multi-core CPU and PassMark database performance.
Features 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Achieves high-throughput Redis SET operations exceeding 61 million ops/sec.
Optimized for database hosting, multi-threaded applications, and caching layers.
